PETITION TO DETERMINE DISTRIBUTION RIGHTS, CALENDAR OF OCT-25-2010 THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SEPTEMBER 9, 2006 CODICIL DEMONSTRATES DECEDENT'S INTENT THAT HER TANGIBLE PERSONAL PROPERTY PASS TO FRED LOVILLE. ACCORDINGLY, THE PROCEEDS OF THE REAL PROPERTY WHICH HAS NOW BEEN SOLD SHOULD BE DISTRIBUTED 50% TO THE ESTATE OF JAMES LOVILLE, SR. AND 50% TO THE ESTATE OF FRED LOVILLE. JAMES LOVILLE JR. SHALL BE REIMBURSED THE SUM OF $8,738.59 AT THE TIME OF FINAL DISTRIBUTION. THE REQUESTS FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. (SEE ORDER THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT).

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Jordan Sussman for Terrence Derek Loville. Robert Ferris for James Loville Sr.

Mr. Ferdon objected to the amount of the fees requested by Mr. Ferris. Mr. Ferris stated that that he wished that the SFPG had filed a written objection so that he could properly respond and presented an explanation of the services provided to support his fee request. Mr. Ferdon responds. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio joins in Mr. Ferdon's argument. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Continued To Mar-08-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Nov-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for and with James Loville III (Former Executor). Jordan Sussman for and with Terrence Loville and for Derek Loville. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator

Mr. Sussman stated his Clients were waiving their opposition to the request for expenditures in the amount of $8,738.59. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the markings on the will should be read as a seperate codicil, the interpertation of the term "worldly goods" and who should be entitled to receive under the will/codicil. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (634 Head Street, San Francisco, Ca 94132)
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Franscisco Public Administrator. Doris Wong (Sales Reprensentative) from Pacific Union Real Estate.

There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $380,000.00 (subject to a written order). Ms. Wong requested that the sale close in forty days. The Court informed Ms. Wong that she would need to speak with Mr. Ferdon. Mr. Ferdon stated he would submit an order and that he would speak with Ms. Wong regarding her request.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for Terrence Loville. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Pubilc Administrator. James Loville (Grandson) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Ferris stated that his Client was not objecting to the Public Administrator ("PA") being appointed and that a Petition To Determine Entitlement needed to be filed by the PA. James Loville stated that he agreed to the property being sold, that he did not have a problem with the PA being appointed and that he was interested in purchasing the property. Mr. Ferdon stated that he wanted to make sure the property was not in foreclosure, that the mortgage being paid was not at market value, that the will had been changed and that the PA would not file the Petition To Determine Entitlement as they were a neutral Party. James Loville agreed to the Court's suggestion of participating in mediation. Mr. Ferris stated that there was no way to avoid a Petition To Determine Entitlement as there were 14 other possible Heirs and that if the will was found to be inavalid, that the estate would be intestate. Mr. Ferdon suggested selling the property and holding the funds until a determination is made. The Court
appointed the San Francisco Public Administrator with authority to sell the property. Mr. Ferdon will prepare the order.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Continued To Aug-26-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for Terrence Loville (Petitioner). James Loville (Grandson) in Propria Persona. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferris stated that the parties had spoken in the past, sugggested the Public Adminisrtator be appointed, stated that a Petition For Entitlement would be filed in the future and that the Parties would be in an adversarial position. In addition, Mr. Ferris requested that James Loville and his Brother vacate the property, return the keys and allow his Client to remove his father's personal belongings. James Loville stated that he should be appointed, that he had brought all of the utility bills current and that he was maintaining the property. Mr. Ferdon stated that he had not reviewed the file, that he wanted to know what the expectations are for the time the Public Administrator would be appointment, that the Parties were to submit what they had and requested a two week continuance. The Court stated that the Public Administrator would be appointed in the future. The matter was continued to August 26, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed both sides to cooperate with the Public Administrator, stated that all claims needed to be supported by documents and to be turned over to the Mr. Ferdon.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Continued To Aug-12-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for Terrence Loville (Petitioner). James Loville in Propria Persona.

This was a pre-granted matter. James Loville appeared and objected to the petition. The matter was continued to August 12, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed James Loville that he needed to submit his objections in writing two weeks prior to the hearing date.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
